name: Sambisari Temple
notes: Discovered 1966 by farmer. 9th century temple buried 5-6m under Merapi deposits.

name: Kedulan Temple
notes: Discovered 1993 by sand diggers. 14 sediment layers, 8m total. Outer fence at 7m depth.

name: Dwarapala Singosari
notes: Photographed 1860 half-buried. Rate: 3.6 mm/yr. Total height 370cm.

name: Borobudur (Lake sediment)
source: Newhall et al. 2000; Murwanto et al.
notes: Not buried but built on lake sediment impounded by Merapi debris avalanche ~3400 BP. Indicator of landscape modification.

name: Sangiran Pedotype 1 (Kalibeng)
notes: Oldest pedotype. Dark humic clay with root traces between laharic breccias. Early Pleistocene.

name: Sangiran Pedotype 2 (Pucangan lower)
notes: Marine-influenced paleosol. Black clay with organic matter.

name: Sangiran Pedotype 3 (Pucangan upper)
notes: Lacustrine paleosol. Associated with H. erectus immigration timeframe.

name: Sangiran Pedotype 4 (Grenzbank)
notes: Grenzbank zone. Key transition layer. H. erectus fossils associated.

name: Sangiran Pedotype 5-8 (Kabuh)
notes: Upper pedotypes. Progressive drying signal. Carbon isotope shift.

name: Merapi stratigraphic section (Newhall)
notes: 10000-year record. Multiple buried surfaces between pyroclastic layers. Oldest: 9630±60 BP.

name: Kelud stratigraphic section
notes: 1300-year record. 10 bed-sets, 12 fallouts, 10 PDC units. 32m total volcanic deposits.

name: Liangan Temple
notes: Discovered 2008. Preserved Mataram-era settlement. Pyroclastic surge from Sundoro volcano.

name: Dieng Temple Complex
notes: Multiple temples partially buried by lahars. Active geothermal area.

name: Semarang subsidence boreholes
notes: Multiple boreholes to 80-100m. Quaternary alluvium over volcanic basement. SPT data available.

name: East Java soft soil study
notes: SPT<10 to 15.5m depth. High organic matter content in some layers.

name: Surabaya N-SPT study
notes: Multiple boreholes. Soft clay and silt 0-10m. Vs30 profiles.

name: Purwosari bridge bore log
notes: Bore log from infrastructure project. SPT data to 15m.

name: Taman Sari Malang bore log
notes: Bore log from Malang area. 15m depth.

name: Candi Ijo
notes: Highest temple on Prambanan plateau. Partial burial by ash deposits. 9th century.

name: Candi Morangan
notes: Discovered 2002 during sand mining. 3.5m burial under lahar deposits. Similar age to Sambisari/Kedulan.

name: Candi Kadisoka
notes: Discovered 1966 near Sambisari. 3m burial. 9th century Hindu temple.

name: Candi Kimpulan
notes: Discovered during campus construction. Two temple structures buried 2m.

name: Lusi mud volcano (Sidoarjo)
notes: Multiple deep boreholes. Quaternary volcanic sediments over Miocene carbonate. >1000m stratigraphic record.

name: Pleret (Mataram Sultanate)
notes: 17th century kraton of Sultan Agung. Partially buried by Opak river flooding + volcanic sediment.

name: Candi Pendem (Tegalrejo)
notes: Name means 'buried temple'. Discovered under 4m lahar deposit. Near Borobudur.
